channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Battle Royale Games Collapse as Sandbox Titles Soar Amid New Trends
item.content:encoded:
It’s finally being confirmed in the data. The world is moving on from battle royale titles and is instead focusing on sandbox games, adopting that sense of freedom and choice over being the last man standing. In a new report from NewZoo, it was confirmed that battle royale games saw a monumental drop of -27% in playtime in 2025 versus 2024.

By contrast, sandbox titles grew a whopping 36% in playtime in the same window. There were also some minor declines in the shooter and role-playing space, while adventure games also saw minor growth.

Survival in a Different Way

It seems to be the new trend these days. We’re seeing survival-sandbox games soar in almost every respect, as players settle in and build a life for themselves in some digital universe, often opposed by a traditional threat: zombies, raiders, the elements, and so on. That’s how most sandbox games work.

Battle royale titles, which saw a boom from 2019 onwards with the likes of Fortnite, Apex Legends, and Call of Duty: Warzone skyrocketing, had a monumental drop across all platforms in 2025. The decline isn’t much of a surprise, as it’s effectively common knowledge that the battle royale experience is dying out.

Even Fortnite is leaning into alternate entertainment to keep fans engaged, standing up the UEFN ecosystem and offering all kinds of games and side modes away from the battle royale core.

It gets even more impressive when you realise that sandbox games are flying up over every other genre despite some major releases in the last year. For instance, Battlefield 6, the best-selling game of last year, a first-person shooter, and ARC Raiders, a transformative extraction shooter that racked up millions of sales, both dropped in 2025.

It’s not a good look for these top-tier battle royale games, which are failing to secure traction going forward. Apex Legends had a 24% drop in playtime in 2025, and Fortnite recorded a 29% drop. Shooters also suffered immensely:

  • Call of Duty: -33%
  • VALORANT: -15%
  • Rainbow Six Siege: -24%
  • Overwatch: -29%
  • Destiny 2: -40%

Even Counter-Strike and Marvel Rivals couldn’t save shooters, and that’s with Marvel Rivals seeing a 546% increase in players in 2025. Although that figure is skewed, given that Marvel Rivals dropped in December 2024.

The numbers for 2026 will be much more interesting to view when they’re released, given that ARC Raiders has blown up and will understandably bolster results.

Are you surprised to learn that battle royale games are disappearing? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about the recently proposed Tarkov changes

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Can You Preload Crimson Desert?
item.content:encoded:
The excitement is really mounting surrounding Crimson Desert, as the upcoming open-world behemoth is almost ready to tackle. It’s the kind of release that fans will call in sick for school or work for, but part of maximising the play time is making sure it’s ready for launch.

Some developers allow you to preload the game before launch, so as soon as the clock strikes release time, the game is ready for you to play from minute one. Many out there will be hoping that Pearl Abyss will offer the same treatment for Crimson Desert, and if you’re one of them, there’s some good news for you down below.

Can You Preload Crimson Desert Before Launch?

Yes, you can preload Crimson Desert before launch, meaning you’ll be able to have the game downloaded and ready for its March 19 launch day.

Crimson Desert is having a simultaneous worldwide release, meaning it’ll go live across all devices everywhere in the world at the same time, regardless of time zones. Therefore, you could be playing it at the crack of dawn or the middle of the day, but it’s certain that it’ll be at the same time as everybody else.

  • More Insider Gaming: Crimson Desert Release Date Countdown

That means that some fans won’t be able to spoil the opening hours for those who might have to wait. Instead, everybody can dive into the world of Pywel together, and here are the exact times you can prepare for that journey.

When Can You Preload Crimson Desert?

You can preload Crimson Desert two days before its official launch, meaning it’ll be available for download on March 17.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Crimson Desert Pre-Order Bonuses, Editions & Prices

As mentioned before, the game will have a worldwide release, so that date might be different depending on where you live. Thankfully, Insider Gaming has broken down all of the preload times across a whole variety of time zones, so you can plan your adventures accordingly:

Are you excited to finally play Crimson Desert? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Crimson Desert content, check out the Crimson Desert launch trailer, as well as whether it’s a sequel or prequel to Black Desert Online.

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Strands #739 Hints and Answers for Today, March 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #739 for Thursday, March 12, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, March 12, 2026

The Strands’ answers to Game #739 all focus on different phrases that mean ‘complete’. Think of terms meaning “in totality.”

General Strands Hints For March 12

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Think cows, but replace “d” with “t.”
  • Hint 2: You go to the doctor for a full and _______ checkup.
  • Hint 3: Star Fox when he wins in Smash Bros: “Mission ________.”

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #739 for March 12, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for March 12, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • COME
  • LATE
  • THERE
  • DYED
  • TOTE

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #739 On March 12, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • UTTER
  • COMPLETE
  • THOROUGH
  • TOTAL
  • VERITABLE
  • SPANAGRAM: DYEDINTHEWOOL

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #739?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on March 13 will be Strands Game #740.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising Set Revealed Featuring Mega Greninja
item.content:encoded:
The latest set in the Pokémon TCG has been officially revealed by The Pokémon Company, with Mega Greninja and Mega Floette starring in the set.

Pokémon‘s TCG is currently in the midst of the Mega Evolution era, introducing a wealth of new Mega Pokémon to the TCG that were first introduced in Pokémon Legends: Z-A, and Mega Greninja is undoubtedly the star of the set.

Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising Releases May 22

While all eyes look ahead to Perfect Order, releasing later this month, the next step on the Pokémon TCG schedule has been revealed with Chaos Rising, set for a worldwide release on May 22, 2026.

The set is largely comprised of cards that featured in the Japanese sets Nihil Zero, which was released in January, and Ninja Spinner, which is releasing on March 13.

Headline cards including SIRs for Mega Greninja, Mega Floette, and Mega Dragalge, alongside IRs for the likes of Froakie, Fennekin and Crobat. However, this is based on the Japanese sets, and it has not been revealed yet which cards will be used for promos.

In total, Chaos Rising features five Mega Evolution Pokémon ex, five Pokémon ex, 11 Illustration Rare, 18 Ultra Rare Pokémon and Trainer Cards, and six Special Illustration Rare Pokémon and Supporter Cards.

Chaos Rising is expected to be the final set before the big Pokémon 30th anniversary, which was revealed last month. For the first time, the set will be released simultaneously in Japan and worldwide.

Pre-orders on the Pokémon Center for Chaos Rising, including the Pokémon Center Elite Trainer Boxes, are expected to take place in the coming weeks, but no precise details have been revealed.

What is your chase card going to be in Chaos Rising? Share your thoughts on the set in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, read about the expected release period for the 30th anniversary set, and find out what happened to humans in Pokopia.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Crimson Desert Devs Have Added Denuvo Anti-Tamper
item.content:encoded:
Pearl Abyss has added Denuvo Anti-Tamper to Crimson Desert, and fans don’t seem to be happy about the decision.

Crimson Desert is all set to release for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC in a week on March 19, 2026, and they have released a launch trailer to build hype today. However, it seems the developers have also added third-party DRM to the game just a week before it arrives.

Crimson Desert Gets Denuvo A Week Ahead of Launch, and Some Fans Aren’t Happy

Spotted by The Gamer, if you head over to SteamDB and see Crimson Desert’s update history, you will see that the latest change today stated: “Added 3rd-Party DRM – Denuvi Anti-tamper, 5 different PC within a day machine activation limit.” This has seemingly sent some fans into a frenzy, with a full-fledged Steam discussion about players disappointed with the decision.

For those unaware, Denuvo Anti-tamper is software designed to prevent games from being pirated or cracked. However, there are claims that this software can affect a game’s performance, including reduced FPS, stuttering, and more. At the same time, some claim that it doesn’t impact the game.

Moreover, Pearl Abyss made several other changes to Crimson Desert’s Steam store listing, including changes to the store description, the trailers, screenshots, and some assets.

In other news, here’s Insider Gaming’s guide on Crimson Desert’s pre-order bonuses, game editions, prices, and more. Additionally, Pearl Abyss has fired back against doubters who claim they’re hiding elements. What are your thoughts on Crimson Desert getting Denuvo a week ahead of launch? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Pokémon Pokopia surpassing 2.2 million global sales in 4 days. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Super Meat Boy 3D Release Date Seemingly Revealed through Nintendo eShop
item.content:encoded:
According to the Nintendo eShop listing of Super Meat Boy 3D, the game is scheduled to release at the end of March 2026.

Team Meat revealed Super Meat Boy 3D during the Xbox Games Showcase last year, confirming the title would release in 2026 for Xbox Series X/S, PlayStation 5, and PC. We also received a brand-new trailer that showcased fresh 3D-perspective gameplay, along with new stages, obstacles, and more. Now, it seems players will be able to play it by the end of this month.

Super Meat Boy 3D Releases on the Last Day of March 2026

If you head over to the Nintendo eShop right now, you will see that Super Meat Boy 3D lists a release date, seemingly March 31, 2026. Now, there is some speculation that this could be a placeholder for the game, as it is the last day of March. However, there’s another side to the story, where players think that the game’s release date might have been accidentally revealed ahead of the official announcement at the Future Games Show later today.

In Super Meat Boy 3D, you get to play as an animated cube of meat, who is trying to save his girlfriend, who is made of bandages from “an evil fetus in a jar wearing a tux.” The game features the old-school difficulty of retro titles, where you get to indulge in “tough-as-nails precision platforming action.” You will explore forests that could be on fire, dumps filled with the waste of mankind, and high-tech forges that are responsible for the traps that kill Meat Boy regularly.

In other news, check out Insider Gaming’s hands-on preview of Super Meat Boy 3D from Gamescom 2025. What are your thoughts on Super Meat Boy 3D’s release date accidentally surfacing online? Do you think it is a placeholder? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Crimson Desert’s launch trailer, showcasing some epic boss fights that players should expect. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
